key_insight: Unified Gradient Projection (UGP) reduces catastrophic forgetting in multilingual low-resource ASR by projecting updates into a language-balanced gradient space that limits dominant-language bias.
engineering_relevance: For continual adaptation of pretrained speech models, UGP offers a practical way to improve stability and preserve prior-language performance while still learning new low-resource languages.
method: Build reference gradients from language-balanced replay data.
Project parameter updates in a unified space to equalize per-language contributions.
Combine gradient-level projection with data replay for complementary stability and plasticity gains.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation across diverse low-resource language groups and model scales, with Whisper-large-v3 achieving near-zero average forgetting.
watch_item: UGP depends on well-constructed language-balanced replay and projection overhead may complicate training and scaling in resource-constrained deployments.
paper_title: Unified Gradient Projection: Language-Balanced Continual Learning for Multilingual Low-Resource ASR
